*   Reheater Temperature: 1240 °C - 1760 °C (2240 °F - 3200 °F)
*   CO2 Compression Pressure: 145 bar (2100 psia)
*   ASU Sizes range: 2600-6700 metric tons O2/day
*   NG & Coal Syngas plants > 200 MWe require exceeding current ASU compressor capacities
*   Conventional ASU limit: About 3600 metric tons O₂/day

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Blade root stresses remain approximately the same [proportional to (speed)^2 and torque]

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

There are two tables implicitly presented.

*   **Baseline Aero-derivative Turbine (150 MWe ZEPP):**

    *   Two-stage design
    *   Inlet pressure = 29.93 bar (434 psia)
    *   Design Speed = 9586 rpm
    *   Design flow rate = 126.0 kg/sec (277 lb/sec)
    *   Compressor pressure ratio = 29.4
    *   Cooling air flow rate 9.31 % of main gas flow
    *   Inlet temperature = 1245 °C(2273 °F)
    *   Exit pressure = 7.03 bar (102 psia)
    *   Speed range = 9500 to 10,800 rpm
    *   Turbine mean diameter 77.47 cm (30.5 inches)
    *   Cooling air temperature 504 °C(940 °F)

*   **Baseline Industrial Gas Turbine (700 MWe ZEPP):**

    *   Four stage design
    *   Inlet Pressure = 19.31 bar (280 psia)
    *   Compressor pressure ratio = 19.1
    *   Design speed = 3600 rpm
    *   Cooling air 232 °C (450 °F) flow rate 4.8 % of turbine design flow rate.
    *   Inlet Temperature = 1427 °C (2600 °F)
    *   Exit Pressure = 1.10 bar (16 psia)
    *   Design flow rate = 583 kg/sec (1282 lb/sec)
    *   Turbine mean dia. = 211 cm (83 inches)

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Design based on 50% reaction
*   Turbine efficiency approximately equal to 90%
*   Blade cooling effectiveness equal to 100%
*   Cooling Temperatures: ~510 °C (~950 °F), ~230 °C (~ 450 °F), 230-270 °C (450-500 °F)
